methods.description: This method describes the process of obtaining, housing, and feeding one-day-old quail chicks, including their individual weighing, random allocation to different dietary treatment groups, and the formulation and administration of experimental diets.

steps.description: The experiment enrolled 256 of one-day-old quail chicks.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain one-day-old quail chicks

steps.description: They were weighed individually and randomly allocated to eight dietary treatments so that each experimental unit had equal average weight and distribution of eight birds.
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh chicks individually and randomly allocate to treatment groups balancing weight and distribution

steps.description: Experimental groups consisting of: a control diet (not supplemented), ethanolic extract of propolis 1000 mg kg -1 (PE1) and 5000 mg kg -1 (PE2), pollen powder 1000 mg kg -1 (PO1) and 5000 (PO2) mg kg -1 , royal jelly 100 (RJ) mg kg -1 , honey 22 g L -1 (H) and virginiamycin 150 mg kg -1 (V).
steps.searchable_summary: Define the specific dietary treatments and their compositions/dosages

steps.description: The quail chicks were kept in cages (40 × 32 cm 2 ).
steps.searchable_summary: House chicks in specified cage dimensions

steps.description: Experimental diets were fed from 1-42 days of age. Feed and water were available ad libitum.
steps.searchable_summary: Administer experimental diets and provide feed and water ad libitum for the duration of the experiment

steps.description: The diets were formulated based on soybean-corn, to meet or exceed NRC requirements. 18
steps.searchable_summary: Formulate diets based on soybean-corn to meet or exceed NRC requirements

steps.description: The experimental groups were replaced with sand in diets during grower periods.
steps.searchable_summary: Replace experimental supplements with sand in diets during grower periods

methods.description: This method describes the process for preparing a 30.00% propolis tincture using 70.00% ethanol.

steps.description: Propolis, pollen, royal jelly and honey were collected of honey bee, Animal Science Research Institute, Karaj, Iran.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect propolis, pollen, royal jelly, and honey

steps.description: Propolis was kept desiccated in the dark until processing.
steps.searchable_summary: Store propolis desiccated in the dark

steps.description: A 30.00% propolis tincture was prepared by adding 600 g propolis to 1400 mL 70.00% ethanol (w/v).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare 30% propolis tincture by mixing propolis and ethanol

steps.description: This was mixed and kept in a glass container, shaken twice daily, filtered after one week and kept at 4 ˚C until it was used. 19
steps.searchable_summary: Mix the tincture, store in glass, shake daily, filter after one week, and store at 4°C

methods.description: This method describes how body weight (BW) and feed intake (FI) were recorded and used to calculate weight gains (WG) and feed conversion ratio (FCR) for different periods of the experiment.

steps.description: Body weight (BW) and feed intake (FI) per replication were recorded on days 21 and 42...
steps.searchable_summary: Record body weight and feed intake per replication on specified days

steps.description: ...and these values were used to calculate weight gains (WG), FI and feed conversion (FCR) of the quail chicks for the periods between days 0-21, 21-42, as well as for the overall experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate weight gain, feed intake, and feed conversion ratio for specified periods

methods.description: This method describes a test performed on day 42 to assess skin swelling response to 1-chloro-2,4-dinitrobenzene (DNCB) as a measure of delayed-type hypersensitivity.

steps.description: On 42 days of age, 1 mg mL -1 DNCB solution (Merck, Darmstadt, Germany) was spread and maintained over a 2 cm 2 area of featherless skin (0.05 mL) on the right side of the two birds per cage.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and apply DNCB solution to a specific skin area on selected birds

steps.description: Similar position on the left side of the bird treated by the solvent alone (acetone: olive oil, 4:1 v/v) to correct the solvent effect.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply solvent alone to the control skin area on the opposite side of the bird

steps.description: The skin swelling was calculated as the difference between the thickness of the skin before and after DNCB treatment was measured using a digital caliper. 20
steps.searchable_summary: Measure skin thickness before and after DNCB treatment using a digital caliper

steps.description: The skin swelling was calculated as the difference between the thickness of the skin before and after DNCB treatment was measured using a digital caliper. 20
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate skin swelling as the difference in thickness before and after treatment (correcting for solvent effect)

methods.description: This method describes injecting phytohemagglutinin (PHA-M) into the wing web of chicks at six weeks of age and measuring the resulting swelling response as an indicator of cell-mediated immunity.

steps.description: At six weeks of age, two chicks from each cage were injected with mitogen phytohemagglutinin (PHA-M; Gibco, Grand Island, USA) into the left wing web, intradermally.
steps.searchable_summary: Select chicks and inject PHA-M intradermally into the left wing web

steps.description: Each chick received 50 μL of a suspension of 50 μg PHA-M in 50 μL phosphate buffered saline. 21
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and administer PHA-M suspension in PBS

steps.description: A digital caliper (Mitutoyo, Kawasaki, Japan) was used to measure the wing web thickness before injection (as a control measurement), and at 24 hr after injection to obtain the wing web swelling in response to the mitogen. 22
steps.searchable_summary: Measure wing web thickness using a digital caliper before and 24 hours after injection
outputs: wing web thickness (24 hr after injection)

steps.description: A digital caliper (Mitutoyo, Kawasaki, Japan) was used to measure the wing web thickness before injection (as a control measurement), and at 24 hr after injection to obtain the wing web swelling in response to the mitogen. 22
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate wing web swelling as the difference in thickness before and after injection
inputs: wing web thickness (24 hr after injection)

methods.description: This method describes the vaccination procedures against Newcastle disease (ND) and Avian Influenza (AI) H9N2, and injection with Sheep Red Blood Cells (SRBC), followed by assessment of antibody levels against these antigens using the Hemagglutination Inhibition (HI) test.

steps.description: Vaccination against Newcastle disease (ND) were performed on day seven using eye drop (live B1 strain; Razi Institute, Karaj, Iran)...
steps.searchable_summary: Vaccinate chicks against ND on day 7 via eye drop

steps.description: ...and AI H9N2 subcutaneous injection on day 21 (Newcastle and avian influenza killed vaccines; Razi Institute) to all chicks.
steps.searchable_summary: Vaccinate chicks against AI H9N2 on day 21 via subcutaneous injection

steps.description: As a booster, the second live ND vaccine (La Sota strain) was performed via drinking water at day 23.
steps.searchable_summary: Administer booster ND vaccine on day 23 via drinking water

steps.description: The immune response was assessed by hemagglutination inhibition (HI) test. 23 The HI titers against both vaccines were determined on serum samples of the same birds at day 42.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect serum samples on day 42 and determine HI titers against ND and AI vaccines

steps.description: The sheep red blood cells (SRBC) were collected and washed three times in phosphate buffer saline (PBS). The packed cells were brought to 5.00% in sterile PBS solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect, wash, and prepare a 5% suspension of sheep red blood cells in PBS

steps.description: Chicks were injected into breast muscle with SRBC (0.1 mL per chick) followed by a booster injection at days 28 and 35, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Inject chicks with SRBC into breast muscle on day 28 and administer a booster injection on day 35

steps.description: Blood samples were drawn on day 42. Serum was stored at -20 ˚C until tested.
steps.searchable_summary: Draw blood samples on day 42 and store serum at -20°C

steps.description: The antibody levels against SRBC were measured by HI test. 24,25
steps.searchable_summary: Measure antibody levels against SRBC using the HI test on stored serum samples

methods.method_name: Body and Lymphoid Organ Weight Measurement
methods.description: This method describes the process of slaughtering birds on day 42, removing and weighing lymphoid organs (bursa and spleen), and calculating the immune organ index.

steps.description: The birds slaughtered on day 42 were eviscerated...
steps.searchable_summary: Slaughter birds on day 42 and eviscerate

steps.description: ...and bursa and spleen were removed and weighed.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove and weigh bursa and spleen

steps.description: The immune organ index was expressed as the weight of the organ as a percentage of the organ to the total body weight.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate immune organ index as organ weight percentage of total body weight

methods.description: This method describes the process of preparing blood smears, staining them, counting leukocytes, and calculating the ratio of heterophils to lymphocytes (H/L ratio) as an indicator of stress.

steps.description: Blood smears for differential leukocyte counts were prepared using the standard two-slide wedge procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare blood smears using the two-slide wedge method

steps.description: The samples were air dried, fixed in methanol and stained with wright quick stain.
steps.searchable_summary: Air dry, fix with methanol, and stain blood smears with Wright quick stain

steps.description: Smears were examined to obtain counts of lymphocytes and granulocytes per 100 leukocytes.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine stained smears under microscope to count lymphocytes and granulocytes per 100 leukocytes

steps.description: Obtained cell counts were used for calculation of the relative proportion of heterophils to lymphocytes (H/L ratio). 26
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the heterophil to lymphocyte (H/L) ratio from cell counts

methods.description: This method describes the statistical procedures used to analyze the experimental data, including the model, software, post-hoc test, and significance level.

steps.description: The data were analyzed by generalized linear model (GLM) procedure for completely randomized experimental design with seven treatments and four replicates using SAS (Version 8.2; SAS Institute, Cary, USA)...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform generalized linear model (GLM) analysis for a completely randomized design with specified treatments and replicates using SAS
inputs: experimental data (performance, immune parameters, organ weights, H/L ratio)

steps.description: ...and the means were compared by Tukey's test.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Tukey's post-hoc test for mean comparison

steps.description: A p value less than 0.05 was considered as statistically significant.
steps.searchable_summary: Set statistical significance level at p < 0.05
